/cwe/weakness/482.Name: Comparing instead of Assigning
/cwe/weakness/482.Description: The code uses an operator for comparison when the intention was to perform an assignment.
/cwe/weakness/482.ExtendedDescription: In many languages, the compare statement is very close in appearance to the assignment statement; they are often confused.

ModesOfIntroduction.Note: This bug primarily originates from a typo.

CommonConsequences.Note: The assignment will not take place, which should cause obvious program execution problems.

Entries.IntroText: The following example demonstrates the weakness.

The following C/C++ example shows a simple implementation of a stack that includes methods for adding and removing integer values from the stack. The example uses pointers to add and remove integer values to the stack array variable.

Entries.BodyText: The push method includes an expression to assign the integer value to the location in the stack pointed to by the pointer variable.

However, this expression uses the comparison operator "==" rather than the assignment operator "=". The result of using the comparison operator instead of the assignment operator causes erroneous values to be entered into the stack and can cause unexpected results.

PotentialMitigations.Description: Many IDEs and static analysis products will detect this problem.
